scenario.context: Station 02 of the day run: project management standards and project organization.
scenario.mission: You select suitable PM standards and structure roles as well as communication paths for a customer project.

customerRequest.subject: Station 02: PM standard and organization
customerRequest.body: Please define the PM approach for a new customer project and describe a viable project organization.

questions.title: Choose a PM standard
questions.prompt: What should the selection of a PM standard be aligned with?

options.rationale: Too narrow for the project context.

options.text: With project complexity, team size, and compliance requirements

options.rationale: A tool does not replace a methodological fit.

feedback.correct: Correct: These are exactly the factors that determine whether the standard fits.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: The standard has to match the project context.

questions.title: Make project organization robust
questions.prompt: Which elements are important for a clear project organization? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Define a communication rhythm for status and risks
Define escalation paths with named contacts

feedback.correct: Correct: The organization is clear and able to act.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please secure responsibilities and escalation more clearly.

questions.title: Build the project organization
questions.prompt: Put the setup of a project organization in order.
items: Clarify the project goal and framework
correctOrder: Clarify the project goal and framework

feedback.correct: Correct: The organizational setup is logical and robust.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please place goal and role clarification before rules and approval.

questions.title: Weekly meeting effort
questions.prompt: Each week, 3 status meetings of 25 minutes each and 1 risk review lasting 40 minutes are planned. How many minutes is that in total?

feedback.wrong: Please add all meeting times correctly.

questions.title: Describe project organization briefly
questions.prompt: Describe a suitable project organization for a customer order in 4-6 sentences.
questions.expectedOutline: Role model, communication logic, and escalation path in a compact structure.

feedback.correct: Correct: The organization is described in a traceable way.
feedback.wrong: Please elaborate roles, communication, and escalation more concretely.
